summary: Removed the remaining project-level `MUST_MATCH` build/config mechanism and converted the remaining conditional code to clearer guards such as `MWERKS_GEKKO` for compiler-specific inline assembly and `!BUGFIX` for original/bug-compatible data retention. The PR also demoted several old inline-assembly `GXTev` matched bodies to `NOT_IMPLEMENTED`, added `__ppc_eabi_init.c` to splits/config as a nonmatching object, and made a few local macro/header cleanups needed after eliminating `MUST_MATCH`. There was no PR body or review discussion in the slice, so intent is inferred from the title and diff.

change_classification.primary_type: tooling/config cleanup for matching mode removal

what_changed: Removed Makefile logic that defined `MUST_MATCH` via `ASFLAGS += --defsym MUST_MATCH=1` and `CFLAGS += -DMUST_MATCH` when `NON_MATCHING` was not set.
Removed the `configure.py --must-match` argument and its `-DMUST_MATCH` C flag injection.
Removed `-D MUST_MATCH` from `tools/m2ctx/m2ctx.py` default preprocessor arguments.
Added `dolphin/os/init/__ppc_eabi_init.c` to `configure.py` as `Object(NonMatching, ...)` and added its `.init` and `.text` ranges to `config/GALE01/splits.txt`.

Replaced `#ifdef MUST_MATCH` with `#ifdef MWERKS_GEKKO` around compiler-specific inline assembly in `src/Runtime/__init_cpp_exceptions.c`, `src/dolphin/os/init/__ppc_eabi_init.c`, `src/dolphin/os/init/__start.c`, and `src/placeholder.h`.
Restructured `__init_cpp_exceptions` so the function always exists, with the MWERKS_GEKKO path registering the fragment with `GetR2()` and the fallback path calling `NOT_IMPLEMENTED`.

Restructured `__start.c` so `__start` and static `__init_registers` have MWERKS_GEKKO asm implementations and non-MWERKS `NOT_IMPLEMENTED` fallbacks; removed the public `__init_registers` prototype from `__start.h`.

Removed large `#ifdef MUST_MATCH` inline-assembly implementations of `GXSetTevSwapMode`, `GXSetTevSwapModeTable`, and `GXSetTevOrder` from `src/dolphin/gx/GXTev.c`, replacing them with normal C function stubs that call `NOT_IMPLEMENTED`.

Changed data/assert retention guards from `defined(MUST_MATCH) || !defined(BUGFIX)` to `#ifndef BUGFIX` in `OSAlloc.c`, `debug.h`, and `mobj.c`; changed a `texp.c` static data block from `#ifdef MUST_MATCH` to `#ifndef BUGFIX`.

Moved `HSD_ASSERT2` out of the shared baselib debug header and defined it locally in `ftCo_DamageIce.c`, apparently because only that file needed the file/line/message assertion form after the header cleanup.
Kept the `#pragma dont_inline` around `HSD_GObj_80390EB8` use in `gobj.c` unconditionally, while converting the old `MUST_MATCH` comments into Doxygen-style `@todo` notes.
smart_moves: Separated two meanings previously overloaded onto `MUST_MATCH`: compiler capability (`MWERKS_GEKKO` for inline asm and special registers) versus original-code/bug-compatible behavior (`!BUGFIX` for force-active strings and assertions).
Removed the compatibility switch from all major entry points at once: Makefile, configure.py, and m2ctx, reducing the chance that future tools see a stale `MUST_MATCH` define.
Preserved callable symbols by using `NOT_IMPLEMENTED` fallbacks instead of deleting nonportable functions outright; this keeps non-Metrowerks/tooling builds structurally coherent.
Made `__init_registers` static in the C file and removed it from the header, reflecting that it is an internal startup helper rather than a public init API.
Moved file-specific assertion macro use into the only changed consumer shown (`ftCo_DamageIce.c`) rather than keeping a broad `HSD_ASSERT2` macro in `debug.h`.
For old matching-only pragma control in `gobj.c`, retained the matching tactic (`#pragma dont_inline`) while documenting the underlying decomp question about possible file boundaries.
decomp_lessons: Do not use a broad project-level `MUST_MATCH` macro to mean both 'build the matching target' and 'this compiler supports target inline asm'; prefer precise guards like `MWERKS_GEKKO` for compiler-specific assembly.

When preserving retail-compatible data or line-number behavior, guard on bugfix policy (`#ifndef BUGFIX`) rather than matching mode. Matching and bug compatibility are related but not identical concerns.

If an inline-assembly body is removed because it depends on the matching compiler, leave a typed C stub with `NOT_IMPLEMENTED` so call sites and headers remain stable while the function awaits real decompilation.
Startup/runtime files may need section-aware configuration updates when split into normal objects; keep `splits.txt` and `configure.py` object lists synchronized.
Inline prevention pragmas can be retained as a matching tactic independent of a global matching macro when evidence suggests inlining changes codegen.
Macro cleanup should minimize global namespace exposure: file-specific helper assertion macros can live near their only use until a better shared API is designed.
naming_conventions: Use `MWERKS_GEKKO` to guard Metrowerks/Gekko-only inline assembly such as `asm`, `SECTION_INIT asm`, `GetR2`, `__init_hardware`, `__flush_cache`, `__start`, and `__init_registers`.
Use `BUGFIX`/`#ifndef BUGFIX` to distinguish original bug-compatible behavior, retained force-active strings, and original assertion line behavior.
Use `NOT_IMPLEMENTED` for portable fallback bodies when the real implementation is only available under MWERKS_GEKKO.
Keep internal startup helpers such as `__init_registers` static when they are not intended for headers.
Doxygen-style TODO references in C comments can link symbols with `#HSD_GObj_80390EB8` and `#HSD_GObj_80391070`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Removed `MUST_MATCH` assembler and C defines from Makefile, configure.py, and m2ctx rather than relying on build mode to expose matching-only code.
Converted inline asm gates from `MUST_MATCH` to `MWERKS_GEKKO`, which directly describes the compiler feature needed for `asm` blocks and register instructions.

Deleted large hand-written asm bodies for `GXSetTevSwapMode`, `GXSetTevSwapModeTable`, and `GXSetTevOrder`; the resulting source intentionally leaves those functions nonimplemented until a C decomp or better matching tactic exists.
Kept `#pragma push`, `#pragma dont_inline on`, and `#pragma pop` around `HSD_JObjDispAll(... HSD_GObj_80390EB8(arg1) ...)` as a local codegen control instead of hiding it behind `MUST_MATCH`.
Kept `#pragma force_active on` blocks under `#ifndef BUGFIX` for unused strings/static data in OSAlloc and MObj, indicating those bytes matter for original-compatible layout or matching.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that implement `GXSetTevSwapMode`, `GXSetTevSwapModeTable`, or `GXSetTevOrder` in C after these asm bodies were removed.
Search for any remaining `MUST_MATCH` references after PR 1180 to confirm the macro was fully retired.
Audit `#ifndef BUGFIX` blocks introduced here to see whether they still represent original-code compatibility or should be replaced by narrower feature macros.
Check later history of `HSD_ASSERT2` to see whether the local `ftCo_DamageIce.c` macro became a shared assertion API.
Review later changes to `__ppc_eabi_init.c` and startup init splitting for lessons on matching `.init` section functions.
